ISEN 302 Syllabus

Section 500 (53801)

Economic Analysis of Engineering Projects

Spring 2026 - College Station

Course Information

End Time: 10:00AM

End Date: 05/05/2026

Meeting Location:  ZACH 344

Credit Hours:  2

Instructor Details

Ashwini Ravindran

Email:  ashwinir@tamu.edu

Office Location:  ETB 4010 (shared office)

Phone:  979 845 5531

Office Hours

By appointment

Catalog Description

Economic Analysis of Engineering Projects. (2-0). Credit 2. Principles of economic

equivalence; time value of money; analysis of single and multiple investments;

comparison of alternatives; capital recovery and after-tax analysis of economic

projects. Prerequisites: Grade of C or better in MATH 152 or MATH 172; also taught

at Qatar campus.

Course Prerequisites

Prerequisite/Corequisite(s):  Grade of C or better in MATH 152 or MATH 172; also

taught at Qatar campus.

Course Learning Outcomes

Upon completion of this course, the learner will be able to:

Make engineering decisions based on an economic analysis of alternatives;

Explain and demonstrate the principles of economic equivalence and time

value of money;

Formulate and solve equations using interest factors and formulas;

Distinguish between nominal and effective interest rates and convert

between them;

Identify and determine constituent cash flows of single and/or multiple

investments;

Examine, diagram, and compare investment alternatives on an economic

basis using appropriate terminology;

Conclude and justify the best investment alternative(s) based on appropriate

analysis;

Develop and construct spreadsheet models to investigate alternatives;

Determine and utilize appropriate capital recovery methods for alternatives;

Compute and summarize after-tax analysis of projects.

Special Course Designation

None

Grading Policy

There would be 3 exams and an optional final exam. Exam 4 (which is the optional

final exam) is provided as an opportunity for improving your grades.

Exam 1  – 25%

Exam 2  – 25%

Exam 3  – 25%

In-Class Quizzes  – 5%

Homework  - 20%

Exam 4 (Optional Final Exam)  - replaces the lowest grade among the 3

exams.

It is important that all the graded tests / assignments are completed on your own, in

accordance with the Aggie Honor Code and the rules specified for each assignment.

You may not discuss the problems with anyone else until after everyone has

finished taking the test/assignment.

Exam Policy:

All exams and in-class quizzes must be taken in-person in class.

In-class exams would be closed book and closed notes. You are allowed a

one-page single-side handwritten cheatsheet (paper size limited to

Letter)  for exams.

In-class quizzes would be closed book and closed notes unless otherwise

specified. Cheatsheets are  NOT allowed  for in-class quizzes.

Cash-flow tables needed for the exam would be provided along with the test

paper and must be returned along with the test paper at the end of the test.

Hand-held calculators are allowed  to be used during exams/quizzes.  No

other electronic devices  can be used during exams/quizzes.

specified. Cheatsheets are  NOT allowed  for in-class quizzes.

Cash-flow tables needed for the exam would be provided along with the test

paper and must be returned along with the test paper at the end of the test.

Hand-held calculators are allowed  to be used during exams/quizzes.  No

other electronic devices  can be used during exams/quizzes.

Mobile phones, tablets, laptops and other interactive electronic devices

may NOT be used to serve as a calculator  unless an exception has been

granted by the instructor for certain quizzes.

If you do not have a calculator or run into an issue, you may be able to

borrow it from Evans Library prior to the test. Plan in advance since

availability may fluctuate based on demand. It might also be easier to borrow

one from a friend.

Regarding Optional Final Exam:

There is no penalty for taking the optional final exam.

The grade for the optional final exam would be used to replace the lowest

grade among the first 3 exams only if it would improve your existing grade. If

you do not do well on the optional exam, the original grades for the first 3

exams would be retained.

You are allowed to take the optional final  ONLY if you have taken all 3

exams . i.e. Exam 1, Exam 2 and Exam 3.

If you have missed either Exam 1, Exam 2 or Exam 3 due to a valid university-

excused absence (must provide verifiable documentation), then the optional

final exam grade would be used to replace the grade for the missing exam. In

this case, the optional final exam grade would not be used to replace any

other test grade.

Grading Policy for Homework, Quizzes and Exams:

You must explain the logic and show the steps involved in calculation to

demonstrate how you arrived at the answer for every numerical problem on

any given assignment. i.e. the steps (including the formula used) must be

written in the test paper that you submit for in-class exams/quizzes and in

attachment you upload for homework. This also applies to multiple choice

questions that have a numerical answer.

If the work submitted does not clearly indicate your reasoning or outline

steps as to how you arrived at the solution, the points for that question would

be reduced to zero. This is done in order to discourage guess work and can

also help with partial credit.

For each assignment problem, you need to indicate the corresponding

question number for each answer on your written submission.

Homework assignments are to be submitted electronically through Canvas. In

case you face an issue accessing Canvas while within the submission

be reduced to zero. This is done in order to discourage guess work and can

also help with partial credit.

For each assignment problem, you need to indicate the corresponding

question number for each answer on your written submission.

Homework assignments are to be submitted electronically through Canvas. In

case you face an issue accessing Canvas while within the submission

deadline, you can e-mail your homework to the instructor/TA within the

deadline.

Policy regarding late submissions have been outlined below.

Late Work Policy

Work submitted by a student as makeup work for an excused absence is not

considered late work and is exempt from the late work policy ( Student Rule 7 ).

Course Specific Late Work Policy

If you miss any graded quiz/test/assignment, you must contact the instructor

as soon as possible with a written authorized excuse. These have to be

discussed with me via e-mail and the student must be able to provide all

supporting documentation that can be verified. Make up assignments will be

given in accordance with University Rules (see Student Rule 7 at

http://student-rules.tamu.edu ).

If you have missed either Exam 1, Exam 2 or Exam 3 due to an university-

excused absence (must provide verifiable documentation), then the optional

final exam grade would be used to replace the grade for the missing exam. In

this case, the optional final exam grade would not be used to replace any

other test grade.

The following instructions apply  ONLY  to Homework Assignments (take-home

submissions which constitute 20% of the final grade):

You can use up to  1 late day per homework  with no penalty , limited to

a maximum of 3 late days during the entire semester . Using 1 late

day would allow you to submit your homework  within 24 hours  of the

original deadline. You  cannot  use more than 1 late day on any

individual homework assignment. You won’t get more than 3 late days in

any given semester, so use them wisely.

Assignment submissions beyond the given deadline are not accepted

unless you are within your limit for late days as outlined below. Hence,

plan on submitting all assignments early in case you happen to

encounter some kind of technical difficulties later on. Remember that it

is your responsibility to ensure that your work has been submitted

properly.

If you use more than 3 late days during the semester, then that

particular homework assignment and any subsequent late homework

submissions would not be given any points.

Late days DO NOT APPLY to quizzes or exams.

In some cases, certain late assignment submissions may be accepted

is your responsibility to ensure that your work has been submitted

properly.

If you use more than 3 late days during the semester, then that

particular homework assignment and any subsequent late homework

submissions would not be given any points.

Late days DO NOT APPLY to quizzes or exams.

In some cases, certain late assignment submissions may be accepted

at the discretion of the instructor. Any intention of using more than 3 late

days for homework in a given semester would require you to produce

verifiable valid documentation for university allowed excuses, including

documentation for the prior 3 days as well – with penalty adjudged on a

case-by-case basis.

Additional Course Information

Regarding Course Materials

Materials such as but not limited to lecture notes, quizzes, homework, practice

exams, exams, etc. that are provided as part of this course are intended solely for

your personal academic use and it is  not allowed  to distribute these materials by

Other Information
